text:
Deep learning (DL) is rapidly changing the way organisations make their evidence-based decisions across the globe. By employing several layers of non-linear transformations on feature mappings through the process of representation learning, deep learning models manage to achieve higher levels of performance and subsequently obviate performance bottlenecks that are generally observed during processing of large amounts of data. In spite of the DL being a multidisciplinary approach and that it is in its early days, surveys and reviews focusing on the present day deep learning models and architectures are limited. This paper reviews in details with examples various existing deep learning models and algorithms used for knowledge extraction from data. It aims at presenting in depth analyses of the strengths and weaknesses of each approach.

Although deep learning models achieve remarkable performances in a large number of applications, the Review shows that the optimisation of efficiency is very application dependent and that many training strategies are necessary. The field still has many ways to go. For instance, the training of the networks can be extremely long and computationally heavy requiring huge training datasets that are usually of high quality. In addition, many DL models are highly sensitive to various forms of attacks such as input attacks (e.g. Poisons attacks), misclassification, optimisation into local minima and need more efficient and robust hyper parameters optimisation and learning algorithms.

Despite the limitations mentioned above, Deep Learning (DL) has demonstrated unprecedented value in diverse applications such as healthcare, education, national security, and industrial automation. While various popular models such as convolutional neural networks (CNN), recurrent neural networks (RNN), generative adversarial networks (GAN), and autoencoders are used in a variety of real-world applications, a huge number of alternative models with the potential to yield significant advancements remain to be investigated. Hybrid models that utilize traditional algorithms to complement individual model weaknesses, as well as capsule networks, are also gaining prominence and may define the future of Deep Learning. This survey acts as a primer for those interested in this domain including stakeholders, developers, and researchers who can utilize it to gain insights into deep learning and associated advancements in the era of Big Data Analytics.
